findings.summary: Exploration sub-components depend on the wide QueryExecutor interface instead of narrow SPI ports
evidence: SingleSeedExplorer.java line 37: constructor takes QueryExecutor (the wide interface extending Closeable, CollocateQueryPort, SketchQueryPort, plus executeCqlQuery, executeDependencyPattern, getTotalFrequency)
MultiSeedExplorer.java line 35: 'public MultiSeedExplorer(QueryExecutor executor)' — same wide dependency
CollocateProfileComparator.java line 36: 'public CollocateProfileComparator(QueryExecutor executor, ...)' — same wide dependency
In contrast, api handlers explicitly use narrow ports: SketchHandlers takes SketchQueryPort (line 42), ConcordanceHandlers takes CollocateQueryPort, CorpusQueryHandlers takes CollocateQueryPort
The executeCollocations method used by exploration classes is defined only on QueryExecutor, not on any SPI port — forcing exploration to import the wide interface
findings.suggestion: Extract an ExplorationQueryPort interface (or extend an existing SPI) that exposes only executeCollocations and executeSurfaceCollocations. Declare SingleSeedExplorer, MultiSeedExplorer, and CollocateProfileComparator against this narrower type, mirroring the SPI discipline already applied in the api layer.

findings.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer Javadoc claims 'thin facade' but the class owns non-trivial logic beyond delegation
evidence: Class Javadoc (line ~42): 'Thin facade wiring SingleSeedExplorer, MultiSeedExplorer, and CollocateProfileComparator for the HTTP exploration layer'
fetchExamples() (line ~170): implements deduplication loop with HashSet<String> seen — not a delegation; this is owned business logic
exploreByRelation() (line ~134): validates collocatePosGroup, throws IllegalArgumentException for OTHER group — an ownership decision, not a passthrough
All 4 service methods wrap IOException in ExplorationException — owned translation logic across the class
Three public constructors (convenience, full-injection, test bypass) plus an internal GrammarConfig-to-nounCqlPattern translation in the full-injection constructor
findings.suggestion: Either rename/redescribe the class as a service class (e.g. 'SemanticFieldService') that owns deduplication, validation, and error-wrapping concerns, or extract the non-delegation logic into helpers so the facade claim becomes accurate.

findings.summary: MultiSeedExplorer and CollocateProfileComparator declare public constructors/methods on package-private classes, while SingleSeedExplorer correctly uses package-private visibility throughout
evidence: MultiSeedExplorer.java line 31: 'class MultiSeedExplorer' (package-private), line 35: 'public MultiSeedExplorer(QueryExecutor executor)' — public constructor

CollocateProfileComparator.java line 27: 'class CollocateProfileComparator' (package-private), line 36: 'public CollocateProfileComparator(QueryExecutor executor, GrammarConfig grammarConfig)' — public constructor; line 54: 'public ComparisonResult compareCollocateProfiles(...)' — public method
SingleSeedExplorer.java line 34: 'class SingleSeedExplorer' (package-private), line 41: 'SingleSeedExplorer(QueryExecutor executor, String nounCqlPattern)' — package-private constructor (no modifier)
On a package-private class, 'public' on members has no practical effect outside the package — the modifier is misleading about the intended access level
findings.suggestion: Remove the 'public' modifier from MultiSeedExplorer's constructor and CollocateProfileComparator's constructor and methods, making them package-private to match SingleSeedExplorer's convention and accurately signal that these are internal algorithm classes.

findings.summary: executeCollocations and executeDependencyPattern silently return empty list for null/empty lemma, while executeSurfaceCollocations throws IllegalArgumentException when the lemma is absent from the pattern
evidence: QueryExecutor.java line 36 Javadoc on executeCollocations: 'Returns an empty list silently if null or empty'
BlackLabQueryExecutor.java line 97-103: 'if (lemma == null || lemma.isEmpty()) { logger.debug("..."); return Collections.emptyList(); }'
BlackLabQueryExecutor.java line ~153: executeDependencyPattern also guards 'if (lemma == null || lemma.isEmpty()) { return Collections.emptyList(); }'
BlackLabQueryExecutor.java line ~198: executeSurfaceCollocations throws IllegalArgumentException: 'lemma not extractable from pattern — ... (got: ' + bcqlPattern + ')'
All three methods are surface-query variants in the same interface; callers expecting consistent behavior must handle both the silent-empty and the throw cases
findings.suggestion: Unify the missing-lemma contract across all three methods: either (a) make executeSurfaceCollocations return an empty list when no lemma attribute is present (consistent with the null-lemma behavior of siblings), or (b) change executeCollocations and executeDependencyPattern to throw IllegalArgumentException on null/empty lemma (consistent with executeSurfaceCollocations). Document the chosen contract in the QueryExecutor interface Javadoc.

findings.summary: SketchHandlers.handleRelationQueryForPattern dispatches by relation ID, not by pattern — the 'ForPattern' suffix misrepresents the routing key
evidence: SketchHandlers.java line 171: 'private void handleRelationQueryForPattern(HttpExchange exchange, String lemma, String relationId, RelationType relationType)'
The parameter is named 'relationId', not 'pattern' — the method looks up a RelationConfig by ID and dispatches the query
Sibling methods handleFullSketchForType, handleDependencySketch all use type-based names that accurately describe their dispatch key
Callers (line 62, 72) pass a deprel string or relation string parsed from the URL path segment — these are IDs, not patterns
findings.suggestion: Rename to handleNamedRelationQuery(HttpExchange, String lemma, String relationId, RelationType) to match the actual dispatch semantics and align with sibling method naming.

findings.summary: SnippetResult (the basic concordance hit without collocate extraction) is named for a text artifact rather than its role as the non-collocate variant of ConcordanceResult
evidence: ConcordanceResult.java line 7-8: 'public sealed interface ConcordanceResult permits SnippetResult, CollocateResult' — two permitted subtypes
SnippetResult.java: 'record SnippetResult(String sentence, int startOffset, int endOffset, String docId)' — no collocateLemma, frequency, or logDice fields
CollocateResult.java: 'record CollocateResult(String sentence, String rawXml, int startOffset, int endOffset, String docId, String collocateLemma, long frequency, double logDice)'

The distinction between the two is that SnippetResult is a raw concordance hit (from executeCqlQuery) while CollocateResult is a scored/collocated hit. The name 'Snippet' does not communicate this distinction — it sounds like a display artifact rather than a query result type.
findings.suggestion: Rename SnippetResult to RawConcordanceResult or BasicHitResult to make the contrast with CollocateResult self-evident: one is a plain position-marked concordance, the other carries collocate scoring data.
